meta.title: Velar P400 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Land Rover Velar P400 engine — AJ20P6 3.0 straight-six petrol. One generation, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 12 vetted Land Rover specialists today.

@graph.name: Land Rover Velar P400 Engine Era Map
Land Rover Velar P400 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Velar P400 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ20P6 engine typically costs £4,500–£7,000 supply only, with the same engine used across the Velar, Range Rover, and Range Rover Sport ranges. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Velar P400 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20P6 straight-six Ingenium petrol is considered significantly more reliable than the diesel AJ20D6. It's rated ★★★★☆ based on current data, but cooling system maintenance — particularly the water pump — is the key to longevity. No catastrophic design flaws have emerged.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Velar P400?

acceptedAnswer.text: Water pump failure, coolant crossover pipe leaks, and occasional timing chain tensioner wear are the most common issues. None typically lead to full engine replacement if caught early — preventative cooling system maintenance is the priority.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Velar P400 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a £20,000–£40,000 Velar P400, a £5,500 recon engine is a manageable 14–28% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. Given the Velar's strong retained values, replacement is usually viable. Scrap only if the vehicle has suffered catastrophic damage well beyond the engine.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Velar's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: For the P400, the engine code is AJ20P6 — it's on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The P400 badge signifies the 3.0-litre turbocharged petrol Ingenium. If you're unsure, tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the AJ20P6's water pump, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the water pump is a straightforward repair costing £500–£1,000. The AJ20P6's water pump is the most common service item on this engine; replacing it as a preventative measure at 60,000 miles is strongly recommended.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Velar P400 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Velar P400 engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Velar P400 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — replace the pump before it fails; far cheaper than overheating damage
Straightforward part replacement; book at next service
If caught early; don't drive with a rattling chain
If head gasket or block damage has occurred, replacement may be cheaper
Not a common AJ20P6 failure; if it occurs, replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Velar P400? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Land Rover service history with documented oil-change intervals
* Water pump replaced at or before 60,000 miles as a preventative measure
* Coolant crossover pipes inspected for signs of brittleness or leaks
* Post-2022 build with any early cooling system software updates applied
avoidIf: * Service history shows repeated coolant top-ups with no diagnosis
* Any evidence of overheating — check the service records for head gasket work
* No record of cooling system maintenance on higher-mileage examples (60k+)
* Engine warning light present with no diagnosis documentation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Velar P400's AJ20P6 is JLR's most refined 3.0 petrol yet — a genuine step forward from the AJ126 supercharged V6. But like all modern engines, it demands the right cooling system maintenance. Buy on service history and proof of water pump replacement. If you're replacing, a reconditioned unit with a 12-month warranty is the sweet spot.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Velar P400 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain tensioner wear)
* Service History — Including which engine era (AJ20P6 is the only P400 engine — verify this before purchase)
* Coolant Level — Is the coolant low with no visible leak? Potential water pump or crossover pipe issue
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any hesitation, lag, or whistling?
* Cooling System Check — Has the water pump been replaced? This is the number‑one preventative item
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 5,000 miles? If so, investigate
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved cooling or fuelling issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures; the P400 should pass easily if well‑maintained

items.label: Most Common Failure
items.value: Water pump and cooling system issues — approximately 40% of AJ20P6 enquiries [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Peak Enquiry Season
items.value: Summer — cooling system issues worsen in hot weather [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Regional Demand
items.value: South East (25%), London (20%), South West (14%) [PATTERN DATA]
